items.title: EUR/CNY: Why China’s Yuan Is Drawing Scrutiny in Europe, What It Means for Euro
items.description: The value of the yuan has become a flashpoint in trade relations between China and the European Union. Many EU policymakers and economists believe the currency is undervalued and a key driver of the b

items.title: Hong Kong Brokers Cling to Lunch Break Exchange Wants to Scrap
items.description: Thousands of stock traders, brokers and other financial professionals in Hong Kong may soon lose their lunch hour — and some aren’t happy about it.

items.title: Thai Bond Rally Gathers Pace as Supply Falls, Inflation Slows
items.description: Thailand’s bonds have beaten their emerging Asian peers over the past month and investors say they look set to extend gains on favorable supply dynamics and slower inflation.

items.title: Gold Holds Gain as Traders Weigh Impact of Oil Risks on Rates
items.description: Gold held a gain as traders monitored threats to energy supply routes that risk stoking inflation and putting pressure on the Federal Reserve to hike interest rates.

items.title: Innolight Seeks Up to $7 Billion in Biggest Hong Kong Listing in Years
items.description: China’s Zhongji Innolight Co. has started taking investor orders for a Hong Kong listing to raise as much as HK$55 billion ($7 billion), the city’s biggest in seven years.

items.title: Yen Slides Past 163 Mark to Fresh Four-Decade Low Against Dollar
items.description: The yen slid past 163 per dollar for the first time since 1986, extending its decline and further testing Japanese authorities’ resolve to intervene.

items.title: KKR Commercial Real Estate Lender Studies Potential Sale, Merger
items.description: A commercial real estate lender managed by KKR & Co. said it’s considering strategic alternatives that could include a merger or sale of the money-losing company.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 22
items.description: Oil extended gains after President Donald Trump played down the prospect of near-term talks with Iran while threatening broader strikes, as risks to global supply spread beyond the Middle East to the

items.title: Asian Stocks Advance on Tech Rally, Oil Climbs: Markets Wrap
items.description: Stocks in Asia climbed for a second day as chipmakers at the heart of the artificial intelligence boom extended their rebound. Oil advanced after renewed escalation in the US-Iran conflict.

items.title: Banks Wrap Up Loan Backing SoftBank’s $5.4 Billion Robotics Bet
items.description: A group of banks wrapped up a loan offering backing SoftBank Group Corp.’s $5.4 billion robotics bet after increasing the size of the US dollar tranche.

items.title: This ETF Tax Loophole Is All the Rage Among Rich Americans
items.description: A bespoke fund is becoming a must-have tool for wealthy investors looking to defer — and sometimes even avoid — capital gains tax.

items.title: Moody’s Lifts Argentina Debt Rating in Fresh Boost to Milei
items.description: Moody’s Ratings upgraded Argentina’s credit score, giving the nation its third sovereign upgrade in less than three months and further validating President Javier Milei’s economic overhaul plan.

items.title: Alaska Airlines Narrowly Beats Estimates as Fuel Costs Stay High
items.description: Alaska Air Group Inc. earnings outlook slightly beat analyst predictions as the airline grapples with rising fuel prices from the war in the Middle East.

items.title: Capital One Profit Beats Estimates as Loan-Loss Provisions Drop
items.description: Capital One Financial Corp., the biggest US credit-card lender, swung to a profit in the second quarter as it set aside less money to cover bad loans than Wall Street had anticipated.

items.title: Subprime Auto Dealer Goes From Covid-Era Star to Near Demise
items.description: America’s Car-Mart Inc. thrived during a ’90s slump, the 2008 financial crisis and the upheaval of Covid-19 by doing one thing well: selling and financing older, high-mileage used cars for cash-strapp

items.title: Treasury Flags Concern Over ‘Potentially Abusive’ Tax Trades
items.description: The US Treasury Department has expressed concern over a number of high-profile tax strategies touted by Wall Street that it says may be “too good to be true.”

items.title: Bitcoin Rallies After Bessent Says Clarity Act at ‘1-Yard Line’
items.description: Crypto markets are stirring after months of listless trading on signs that Washington is moving toward a clearer rulebook for digital assets.

items.title: Nasdaq 100 Notches Best Day in Weeks as Chipmakers Rebound
items.description: The Nasdaq 100 Index posted its best day in three weeks on Tuesday as a rebound in chipmakers at the center of the artificial-intelligence trade overshadowed the re-escalation in the US-Iran war.

items.title: High Yielders Pace Emerging-Market Currency Gains, Stocks Jump
items.description: Emerging-market currencies were mixed on Tuesday, with high-yielding names outperforming as investors looked past the latest developments in the Middle East and focused on carry trades instead.

items.title: Stocks Climb as Rebound in Chipmakers Gathers Pace: Markets Wrap
items.description: Wall Street staged a comeback as dip buyers emerged, boosting stocks amid a rally in chipmakers on bets the artificial-intelligence trade that’s powered the bull market has more room to run.
